title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Go Build Room
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a capacity-aware Go work distribution service, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a capacity-aware Go work distribution service while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 9347 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Default Exchange Coupling.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Default Exchange Coupling, keep Default Exchange Coupling connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.
